scenario: Crestwave Marketplace processes 4000 orders per day in Camunda 8.8 and is selecting BPMN task symbols for its order-processing flow. A modeling lead wants to confirm which task variant on the BPMN coverage page is documented without the implemented marker so the team avoids modeling around an unsupported element.
question: Which BPMN task variant is shown on the Camunda 8.8 BPMN coverage page without the implemented marker?

options.text: Send tasks appear on the BPMN coverage page without the implemented marker and link to a placeholder anchor on the page
Instantiated receive tasks appear on the BPMN coverage page without the implemented marker and link to a placeholder anchor
Business rule tasks appear on the BPMN coverage page without the implemented marker and link to a placeholder anchor on the page
Manual tasks appear on the BPMN coverage page without the implemented marker and link to a placeholder anchor on the page

a.text: Incorrect. The send task symbol on the BPMN coverage page carries the implemented marker and links to the dedicated send-tasks documentation page.

b.text: Correct. The instantiated receive task variant is the only task symbol on the BPMN coverage page that lacks the implemented marker and links to a placeholder anchor rather than a dedicated documentation page.

c.text: Incorrect. The business rule task symbol on the BPMN coverage page carries the implemented marker and links to the dedicated business-rule-tasks documentation page.

d.text: Incorrect. The manual task symbol on the BPMN coverage page carries the implemented marker and links to the dedicated manual-tasks documentation page.

explanation: On the BPMN coverage page, service, user, receive, send, business rule, script, manual, and undefined task symbols are all marked as implemented; the instantiated receive task variant is the only task symbol shown without the implemented marker, indicating it is not available on the Camunda 8.8 Zeebe engine.
